title: Applying Semantic Chunking
concept: Splitting text into chunks dynamically using embedding distance between adjacent sentences.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of layout-aware PDF ingestion?

options.text: To extract text while preserving the layout

exercises.explain: The main goal of layout-aware PDF ingestion is to extract text while preserving the layout, which helps maintain the structure of the document.

cards.title: What is Semantic Chunking?
cards.body: Semantic chunking is breaking text into meaningful parts, or 'chunks', to better understand and analyze content.

cards.title: Definition of Semantic Chunking
cards.body: It’s the process of splitting large text into shorter phrases or sentences based on their meaning and relationships.

cards.title: Chunking is Like Puzzles
cards.body: Just like assembling a puzzle with pieces that fit together, semantic chunking helps connect and understand pieces of text.

cards.title: Example of Semantic Chunking
cards.body: Consider the sentences: 'The cat sat on the mat. It was sunny outside.' These can be chunked into: 'The cat sat on the mat.' and 'It was sunny outside.'

cards.title: Myth: Chunking is Random
cards.body: Some think chunking is random, but it's based on meaning and connections, which makes it effective for comprehension.

cards.title: Visualizing Chunking
cards.body: This image shows how sentences can be grouped into meaningful chunks based on their connections.

left.text: 1. "The cat sat on the mat."  2. "It was sunny outside."  3. "The dog barked."

right.text:
Chunk 1: "The cat sat on the mat."
Chunk 2: "It was sunny outside."
Chunk 3: "The dog barked."

exercises.prompt: What does semantic chunking help us with?
exercises.explain: Semantic chunking helps us better understand and analyze text by breaking it into meaningful parts.

exercises.prompt: Semantic chunking is only necessary for poetry and stories.
exercises.explain: False. Semantic chunking is useful for many types of text, not just poetry and stories.

exercises.prompt: Semantic chunking helps break sentences into shorter, more __________ parts.
exercises.sentence: Semantic chunking helps break sentences into shorter, more manageable parts.
exercises.explain: The word 'manageable' fits because it describes how chunking makes sentences easier to work with.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following sentences is an AI-generated chunk?

options.text: The sun rises in the east and sets in the west.
After the rain, the grass was wet.

exercises.explain: The third option is an AI-generated chunk formed to convey a complete idea.

exercises.prompt: Which method does semantic chunking use to determine when to break up sentences?
exercises.explain: Semantic chunking uses embedding distance to identify how closely related sentences are. If they are far apart in meaning, it's a signal to break them into chunks.
